Email. Look, email is one of my least preferred ways of follow-up, but it's still necessary. This is what I call the lazy follow-up, but because everybody has an email address, it's necessary. I'll tell you why in a second. I think it's lazy and I think it's weak, but it's necessary. People think, number one, one of the weaknesses of it, the salesperson or the company believes they followed up because they sent an email. Look at the email didn't get to them, you didn't send it. Remember the old adage, if a tree falls in the forest and nobody's there, did it make a sound? I don't know, but I do know this. If you send an email and it goes to spam, nothing happened. Nobody heard anything, the message wasn't completed, there is no communication, therefore there is no follow-up. I hate emails. Did I make that clear? Freaking hate them. You know why? Because I don't know for sure whether they got them, which means I'm left in doubt. And the reason I do all this follow-up is so I can come out of doubt. I don't want to be in mystery. Also, emails are forgotten very quickly. They get lost in this thing called emails. Today I got, I don't know, 60 in the last 45 minutes. They get all mumbled, jumbled with everything else. Who texts me? My friends do. People that don't know me. People that are close to me text me. Everybody emails me. Also, when you send an email, okay, because so many disadvantages, there's some things you need to embed in that email that can help you. Number one, always a called action. Please call me back right away and phone number. That phone number should go back to your cell number, not to your office. This is a huge mistake companies make. Number one, there's no called action in the email. There's no big claims. There's no promises. They're too long. And most importantly, what's unbelievable to me is there's never a called action and there's never a cell number to contact. I don't want the people emailing me back. I already told you I hate email, but I got to do it because it's necessary because everybody's got one. All right. So you got the point? The point is this. First texts, always email, know it's weak, know it's lazy. No it's not predictable. You can't really measure it, but you got to use it. Oh, by the way, did I say I hate emails? I do. But let me tell you one other thing you can do to make it work. Call them when you email them. When you hit that email and you hit send, now pick up the phone. Okay. You want to hammer down, you want to increase your follow, pick up the phone right now and say, I just sent you an email, take a look at it, call me back if you have any questions. By the way, I feel a little better about emails now.
